title: Types of Discontinuity
statement:
Let $A \subseteq \mathbb{R}$, let $f:A \to \mathbb{R}$, and let $x_0 \in A$ with $f$ discontinuous at $x_0$.
- [label=(\roman*)]
- \emph{Removable} iff $\exists L \in \mathbb{R}:\;\lim_{x \to x_0} f(x) = L$ and $L \ne f(x_0)$.
- \emph{Jump} iff $\exists L_{-}, L_{+} \in \mathbb{R}:\;\lim_{x \to x_0^-} f(x) = L_{-}$, $\lim_{x \to x_0^+} f(x) = L_{+}$, and $L_{-} \ne L_{+}$.
- \emph{Essential} iff it is not removable.
